title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
summary: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) are a paradigm for inference-time compute that can be applied to coding agents to improve performance and reliability. The presentation explores how RLMs work, demonstrates their advantages, and explains how frameworks like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse enable recursive problem-solving in agentic systems.
writeup: Raymond Weitekamp of OpenProse presents Recursive Language Models (RLMs), a paradigm for inference-time compute applied to coding agents. The talk covers how RLMs work, demonstrates their advantages for performance and reliability, and explains how frameworks including Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse enable recursive problem-solving in agentic systems. The framing situates RLMs within test-time compute and reasoning as a method for structuring agent behavior.
